privileged_info_summary: The cointegration_income_consumption task uses real macroeconomic data from the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) system. The two series are PCEC96 (Real Personal Consumption Expenditures) and DSPIC96 (Real Disposable Personal Income), both measured in Billions of Chained 2017 Dollars with Seasonally Adjusted Annual Rate adjustments. However, the task description claims the data are quarterly from 1947Q1 to present, but the actual data downloaded via the provided URLs are MONTHLY frequency: PCEC96 runs from January 2007 to December 2025 (228 months), and DSPIC96 runs from January 1959 to December 2025 (804 months). Quarterly equivalents do exist in FRED (PCEC and DPIC96) but are NOT what the setup script downloads. The data is real (not synthetic) and sourced from the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis via FRED.
